text.title: rust-error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-error-handling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling .unwrap() or .expect() on Result or Option values in non-test production code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: .unwrap() panics at runtime on Err/None. In production, this terminates the process without cleanup. Use ? for propagation or handle the None/Err case explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Silently ignoring errors with let _ = fallible_operation().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent discards hide failures that should be logged or propagated. At minimum, log with error!("{}", e); or propagate with ?. The let _ = pattern is acceptable only when the error is genuinely irrelevant (e.g., closing a channel that may already be closed).

failure-mode.description: Returning Result<(), String> or Err("something went wrong".to_string()) from functions that callers need to handle.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: String errors lose the original error type, prevent match-based handling, and destroy the error chain. Use typed enums or anyhow::Error.
